title: ds-viz-area - option matrix
description: Test bench for splunk.area. Each panel exercises a distinct option group: stacking (auto/stacked/stacked100), opacity, showLines, dual y-axis, annotations, log+null handling, split sub-charts, and the sparkline pattern. SPL is makeresults-based so this dashboard runs in any Splunk instance with no setup. The same time-axis convention used for splunk.line applies: hide x-axis title (the word _time is noise), keep timestamp labels visible (auto), hide major tick marks.

options.query: | makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval requests = round(100 + 30*sin(i/3) + random()%20) | table _time requests

| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval us_east = round(80 + 20*sin(i/4) + random()%10), us_west = round(60 + 25*sin(i/5+1) + random()%10), eu_west = round(45 + 15*sin(i/3+2) + random()%8) | table _time us_east us_west eu_west

| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval frontend = round(40 + 15*sin(i/4) + random()%8), backend = round(60 + 20*sin(i/5+1) + random()%10), database = round(30 + 12*sin(i/3+2) + random()%6) | table _time frontend backend database

| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval mobile = round(35 + 10*sin(i/4)), desktop = round(45 + 8*sin(i/5+1)), tablet = round(20 + 5*sin(i/3+2)) | table _time mobile desktop tablet

ds_dualaxis.name: dual axis - request volume area and p95 line overlay

options.query: | makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval requests_per_min = round(800 + 200*sin(i/4) + random()%80), p95_latency_ms = round(120 + 80*sin(i/3+1) + random()%30) | table _time requests_per_min p95_latency_ms

| makeresults count=30 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-30d@d") + (i*86400) | eval revenue = case(i<10, round(8000+random()%500), i>=10 AND i<20, round(15000+1500*sin(i/2)+random()%800), 1==1, round(11000+random()%600)) | table _time revenue

| makeresults count=3 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = case(i=1, relative_time(now(), "-21d@d"), i=2, relative_time(now(), "-14d@d"), 1==1, relative_time(now(), "-7d@d")), annotationLabel = case(i=1, "Black Friday", i=2, "Christmas Push", 1==1, "Boxing Day"), annotationColor = case(i=1, "#FF2D95", i=2, "#FFB627", 1==1, "#7AA2FF") | table _time annotationLabel annotationColor

| makeresults count=30 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-30d@d") + (i*86400) | eval traffic = round(pow(10, 1+(i/6)) + random()%100), errors = if(i%5=0, null(), round(pow(10, (i/8)) + random()%5)) | table _time traffic errors

| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval cpu_pct = round(40 + 30*sin(i/4) + random()%10), memory_gb = round(8 + 4*sin(i/5+1) + random()%2*0.1, 1), disk_iops = round(2000 + 1500*sin(i/3+2) + random()%500) | table _time cpu_pct memory_gb disk_iops
| makeresults count=40 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-40h") + (i*3600) | eval pulse = round(50 + 30*sin(i/3) + random()%10) | table _time pulse
| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval target = 100, actual = round(70 + 40*sin(i/3) + random()%20) | table _time target actual

| makeresults count=48 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-48h") + (i*3600) | eval signal = round(100 + 50*sin(i/6) + random()%15), baseline = 100 | table _time signal baseline

options.markdown:
# `splunk.area` - option matrix

Each panel exercises one option group. **Convention for every time-series panel:** `xAxisTitleVisibility:"hide"` (the word `_time` is noise), `xAxisLabelVisibility:"auto"` (timestamps stay visible), `xAxisMajorTickVisibility:"hide"` (no tick marks under the axis). **Area-specific:** `stackMode` (`auto` / `stacked` / `stacked100`), `areaOpacity` (0-1, default 0.75), `showLines` (top-edge stroke).

viz_basic.title: 1. Minimal - clean defaults applied
viz_basic.description: Bare options: x-title hidden, x-labels auto, tick marks hidden. Default opacity 0.75, default stackMode auto.

viz_unstacked.title: 2. Multi-series unstacked - stackMode auto
viz_unstacked.description: stackMode default (auto) leaves each series at its own y-position. Areas overlap visually - hard to read with >2 series. Reach for stacked or stacked100 instead.

viz_stacked.title: 3. Stacked - cumulative volume
viz_stacked.description: stackMode=stacked. Each series sits on top of the previous one. The line at the top represents the SUM. Use to show contribution to a total.

viz_stacked100.title: 4. Stacked 100% - share of total
viz_stacked100.description: stackMode=stacked100. Each timestamp normalises to 100%. Use to show how proportions shift over time. Y-axis labels should always be % - we override yAxisTitleText accordingly.

viz_low_opacity.title: 5. Low opacity - readable overlap
viz_low_opacity.description: areaOpacity=0.25 + lineWidth=2.5 keeps lines crisp while letting overlapping areas remain readable. The opposite of panel 2: same data shape, much easier to read.

viz_no_lines.title: 6. No top-edge stroke - showLines false
viz_no_lines.description: showLines=false drops the line on top of each area. Cleaner for stacked-100 and very busy layouts; less useful when readers need to follow individual series boundaries.

viz_dualaxis.title: 7. Dual y-axis - overlayFields + y2
viz_dualaxis.description: Volume series rendered as area on left axis. p95 latency mapped to right axis via overlayFields - it stays a line, never an area. Most common pairing: volume + percentile.

viz_annotations.title: 8. Event annotations - separate annotation dataSource
viz_annotations.description: Annotation marks come from a SECOND dataSource named 'annotation'. Required fields: _time, annotationLabel, annotationColor. Same syntax as splunk.line.

viz_log_nulls.title: 9. Log scale + null handling
viz_log_nulls.description: yAxisScale=log compresses orders of magnitude. nullValueDisplay=connect bridges gaps in the errors series. Minor gridlines OFF on log scale (would produce one line per minor tick).

viz_split.title: 10. Split series - independent y-ranges
viz_split.description: showSplitSeries=true stacks each series in its own sub-chart. showIndependentYRanges=true gives each its own y-axis range. Equivalent to small multiples for area.

viz_hidden_chrome.title: 11. Hidden chrome - sparkline (everything off)
viz_hidden_chrome.description: Sparkline pattern: titles hidden, BOTH axis labels hidden, tick marks hidden, gridlines off, legend off. The shape is the only signal. Use inside KPI tiles or executive cards.

viz_smart_axis.title: 12. Smart axis - recommended baseline
viz_smart_axis.description: Clean defaults pattern. x-title hidden (no '_time'), x-labels auto (timestamps visible), x major ticks hidden. y-axis title carries the unit, baseline drawn as a thin reference area at low opacity.
